name: uvicorn3
documentation:
# uvicorn33

uvicorn的国产化平替版本

classes.name: uvicorn3.middleware.proxy_headers.ProxyHeadersMiddleware
documentation:
Middleware for handling known proxy headers

This middleware can be used when a known proxy is fronting the application,
and is trusted to be properly setting the `X-Forwarded-Proto` and
`X-Forwarded-For` headers with the connecting client information.

Modifies the `client` and `scheme` information so that they reference
the connecting client, rather that the connecting proxy.

References:
- <https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Headers#Proxies>
- <https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Headers/X-Forwarded-For>

classes.name: uvicorn3.middleware.proxy_headers._TrustedHosts

methods.name: uvicorn3.middleware.proxy_headers._TrustedHosts.get_trusted_client_host
documentation:
Extract the client host from x_forwarded_for header

In general this is the first "untrusted" host in the forwarded for list.
Container for trusted hosts and networks

functions.name: uvicorn3.middleware.message_logger.message_with_placeholders
documentation:
Return an ASGI message, with any body-type content omitted and replaced
with a placeholder.

classes.name: uvicorn3.workers.UvicornWorker

methods.name: uvicorn3.workers.UvicornWorker._install_sigquit_handler
documentation:
Install a SIGQUIT handler on workers.

- https://github.com/encode/uvicorn/issues/1116
- https://github.com/benoitc/gunicorn/issues/2604

A worker class for Gunicorn that interfaces with an ASGI consumer callable,
rather than a WSGI callable.

documentation: Shared servers state that is available between all protocol instances.

functions.name: uvicorn3.middleware.wsgi.build_environ
documentation: Builds a scope and request message into a WSGI environ object.

variables.name: uvicorn3.middleware.wsgi.WSGIMiddleware
documentation: type: ignore[misc, assignment]

methods.name: uvicorn3.protocols.websockets.websockets_impl.WebSocketProtocol.process_request
documentation:
This hook is called to determine if the websocket should return
an HTTP response and close.

Our behavior here is to start the ASGI application, and then wait
for either `accept` or `close` in order to determine if we should
close the connection.

methods.name: uvicorn3.protocols.websockets.websockets_impl.WebSocketProtocol.process_subprotocol
documentation:
We override the standard 'process_subprotocol' behavior here so that
we return whatever subprotocol is sent in the 'accept' message.

methods.name: uvicorn3.protocols.websockets.websockets_impl.WebSocketProtocol.ws_handler
documentation:
This is the main handler function for the 'websockets' implementation
to call into. We just wait for close then return, and instead allow
'send' and 'receive' events to drive the flow.

methods.name: uvicorn3.protocols.websockets.websockets_impl.WebSocketProtocol.run_asgi
documentation:
Wrapper around the ASGI callable, handling exceptions and unexpected
termination states.

modules.name: uvicorn3._subprocess
documentation:
Some light wrappers around Python's multiprocessing, to deal with cleanly
starting child processes.

functions.name: uvicorn3._subprocess.get_subprocess
documentation:
Called in the parent process, to instantiate a new child process instance.
The child is not yet started at this point.

* config - The Uvicorn configuration instance.
* target - A callable that accepts a list of sockets. In practice this will
           be the `Server.run()` method.
* sockets - A list of sockets to pass to the server. Sockets are bound once
            by the parent process, and then passed to the child processes.

functions.name: uvicorn3._subprocess.subprocess_started
documentation:
Called when the child process starts.

* config - The Uvicorn configuration instance.
* target - A callable that accepts a list of sockets. In practice this will
           be the `Server.run()` method.
* sockets - A list of sockets to pass to the server. Sockets are bound once
            by the parent process, and then passed to the child processes.
* stdin_fileno - The file number of sys.stdin, so that it can be reattached
                 to the child process.

classes.name: uvicorn3.logging.ColourizedFormatter
documentation:
A custom log formatter class that:

* Outputs the LOG_LEVEL with an appropriate color.
* If a log call includes an `extra={"color_message": ...}` it will be used
  for formatting the output, instead of the plain text message.
